claimLedger.claim: A dated local Codex desktop baseline exposes the recorded CLI, package, Skill-root, and permission metadata.

conditions.model: runtime-declared GPT-5 family; exact build unavailable
conditions.reasoning: not observable from the metadata probes
conditions.loader: selected Skill bodies can be loaded; deterministic implicit loading is unproven
conditions.permissions: repository-scoped workspace write and read-only local capability inspection

supports: local path, file-count, hash, package, and declared-scope observations
claimLedger.counterexample: Claude, Trae, WorkBuddy, Copilot, ChatGPT, Grok, another Codex version, or another account may expose different roots and behavior.
claimLedger.recheckTrigger: host, model, reasoning, loader, permission, workspace, account, or version changes

claimLedger.claim: The dated local snapshot contains 422 SKILL.md files across four observed roots and 87 duplicate directory-name groups.

conditions.host: OpenAI Codex desktop filesystem observation on Windows

claimLedger.counterexample: Two same-name directories can be identical, inactive, or resolved by a host-specific precedence rule.
claimLedger.recheckTrigger: root contents, configured roots, loader, CC Switch, or host precedence changes

claimLedger.claim: A bounded Codex advisory Hook policy and host profile were present with declared auto mode and no declared external mutation.

conditions.activation: declared auto; effective activation not checked
conditions.workspace: user Hook metadata observed from the bound repository task

claimLedger.counterexample: The Hook can be present but not loaded, invoked, consumed, or followed by the model.
claimLedger.recheckTrigger: Hook path, policy, host profile, mode, event delivery, runtime, or behavior probe changes

claimLedger.claim: All 105 deterministic routing scenarios pass the current structured policy contract.
supports: policy decision consistency after structured fact normalization
claimLedger.counterexample: A live Agent can normalize intent differently, skip the router, or fail to follow the returned decision.
claimLedger.recheckTrigger: routing policy, scenarios, normalization contract, host integration, or live evidence changes

claimLedger.claim: The exact pinned Loopy body produced correct boundary-preserving results in the authorized 12-run disposable trial and showed no material benefit over both baselines.

conditions.model: the trial's bound Agent configuration only
conditions.reasoning: trial configuration only; not a weak-model or cross-model matrix
conditions.permissions: one-time bounded candidate execution with no persistence

supports: reference-only disposition for the exact trial conditions
claimLedger.counterexample: Another model, prompt, task class, context budget, or host can produce a different cost or benefit result.
claimLedger.recheckTrigger: new paired behavior, cost, task, model, host, or source-revision evidence

claimLedger.claim: The repository can structurally compress governed routing evidence into a small decision package.
supports: bounded package size and decision-class coverage
claimLedger.counterexample: A consumer can ignore the projection or incur higher integration and context cost than the structural proxy predicts.
claimLedger.recheckTrigger: consumer-owned use, burden, collision, latency, maintenance, or benefit evidence

claimLedger.claim: Public discovery and source-pinned tree preflight establish repository identity, revision, structure, and preliminary review surfaces.

conditions.host: GitHub public metadata and repository-tree inspection
conditions.permissions: public read-only metadata and source inspection

supports: source identity, structure, review cost, and next-gate selection
claimLedger.counterexample: A clean tree with a license file can still contain unsafe, ineffective, incompatible, or misleading instructions.
claimLedger.recheckTrigger: source revision, license, executable surface, maintainer, availability, or bound demand changes

claimLedger.claim: One pinned codex-user-config consumer revision recorded a verified 19-Skill installation and a three-candidate lifecycle feedback cycle.

conditions.reasoning: not recorded as a comparison variable
conditions.activation: installation and routing-index verification, not every Skill invocation

supports: one consumer feedback return path and historical installation verification
claimLedger.counterexample: The consumer may have changed since the pinned revision, and another Agent can use different roots, ownership, precedence, or restore behavior.
claimLedger.recheckTrigger: current consumer mapping, release pin, install, loader, backup, restore, or live-use evidence is proposed

claimLedger.claim: Eight deterministic fixtures validate the repository lifecycle policy's positive and negative transition guards.
supports: policy support for retain, supersede, deprecate, rollback, retire, and required guards
claimLedger.counterexample: A real consumer can fail during backup, migration, verification, rollback, or restore despite the policy fixture passing.
claimLedger.recheckTrigger: a live authorized consumer lifecycle event or policy change

decision.scope: claim-governance firewall only; cross-Agent behavior, parity, loader behavior, Hook effectiveness, and consumer coverage remain evidence-bound and unproven where stated
